denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 04/14/2026
Cigna Open Access Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W101591719          Group #: GRP-1201
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-131558
Claim #: CLM-2026-198654
Date of Service (requested): 03/25/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination regarding prior authorization. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 03/27/2026 for Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy related to Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: Coverage Policy 101 under Cigna Medical Coverage Policy and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG)

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
This determination is based on a plan exclusion. State coverage mandates do not alter the terms of this plan for this determination.
Our review applied the criteria in Coverage Policy 101 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination. If this denial is upheld after internal review, you may have the right to request an independent external review of our decision at no cost to you, subject to applicable federal and state requirements. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W101591719) and reference PA-2026-131558. 
Funding: This determination applies under your employer's self-funded group arrangement (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
Medical Review Unit
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Stage IB grade 3; LVSI present on pathology. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: The brief matches the assigned Cigna / oncology cell and executes sub_tactic 'missing_peer_to_peer' with a clinically appropriate Endometrial cancer, high-grade (C54.1) → Adjuvant vaginal brachytherapy pair.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Diagnosis, prior treatments, and insurer administrative posture reflect everyday commercial UM denials rather than textbook perfection.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Cigna correspondence with MCG Care Guidelines and procedural tone.
